analysis.reasoning: Core message: The EU aims to be climate-neutral by 2050 and coordinates 10-year national energy and climate plans across member states covering efficiency, renewables, emissions, interconnections, and R&I under the #EUGreenDeal. Evaluation: EU1 (successful story): Not about past achievements or history; it's forward-looking policy, so not clearly promoted. EU2 (EU is united): Mentions EU countries preparing plans "to reach our climate targets together," implying coordinated, collective action among member states; this supports a unity framing. EU3 (useful for its citizens): Highlights concrete EU-level planning and measures (efficiency, renewables, emissions, interconnections, research) to meet climate targets, suggesting practical, beneficial policies within the EU; aligns with usefulness. EU4 (avant-garde political actor): Climate-neutral by 2050 and structured planning positions the EU as a pioneer in tackling climate change; fits avant-garde leadership. EU5 (global champion of fair causes): Not about rights or democracy; not applicable. EU6 (contributes to a better world): While climate action has global impact, the tweet focuses on internal EU plans; insufficient emphasis on global contribution, so not selected. Conclusion: EU2, EU3, and EU4 are promoted.
